title: matthiola livida var. incisa
intro: Matthiola livida var. incisa, also known as the cut-leaved stock or stock, is a flowering plant in the Brassicaceae family native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Matthiola livida var. incisa include its ability to relieve congestion, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as an infusion or tincture, and can also be dried and used as a tea or powder for medicinal purposes.
